title: Lesson 03: Your First Action Verbs
description: Transition from identifying objects to describing actions using the present tense and the SOV framework.
author: Poha Editorial

content.heading: Subject + Object + Action
lines: Now we apply the SOV rule to real actions. In English we say 'I drink tea' (SVO). In Pashto, we say 'I tea drink' (SOV).
Let's look at two essential verbs for the first person 'I' (Ze):
Drink: Tskam [Tsk-um] (Note: The 'ts' is pronounced like the end of 'cats')

content.heading: Putting It Together
lines: I eat bread: Ze dod-day khoram. [Zuh doad-day kho-rum]
I drink tea: Ze chay tskam. [Zuh ch-eye tsk-um]
I drink water: Ze oba tskam. [Zuh oh-ba tsk-um]

content.text: Fluency Tip: Pashto is a 'pro-drop' language. Because the verb ending '-am' already tells us the subject is 'I', native speakers often drop the word 'Ze'. You can simply say 'Chay tskam' to mean 'I drink tea'.

content.question: Translate the following to Pashto: 'I eat bread'.
content.explanation: The order must be Subject (Ze) + Object (dod-day) + Verb (khoram).
